glossary.gambling: 赌博 (Only when literal context requires negative tone, otherwise use 游戏)
glossary.free credit: 免费信用 / 免费积分

rules: In Pai Gow and similar card games, when translating 'four of a kind' with specific ranks (e.g., 'four sixes', 'four sevens through tens'), ALWAYS use the pattern '拿到 [numeral/range] 的四条' (e.g., '拿到6的四条', '拿到7到10的四条'). Use the verb '拿到' (get/hand) instead of '有' (have), and use Arabic numerals (6, 7, 10) instead of Chinese number words (六个, 七个).
In Pai Gow, the phrase 'set your hand' or 'set hand' refers to the action of arranging cards into high and low hands. ALWAYS translate this as '摆牌' or '分牌'. NEVER translate it as '设置手牌' or '设置牌'.

In Pai Gow, the instruction 'play [X] in your high/low hand' means placing the specific hand [X] into the designated high or low hand. ALWAYS translate this as '把 [X] 放在高/低牌那手'. NEVER use '打' (play) or '玩' (play) in this context.

In Three Card Poker and Ultimate Texas Hold'em, the term 'straight' (顺子) is a specific hand ranking. NEVER translate 'straight' as '一对' (pair) or '位置' (position). 'Straight or better' must be translated as '顺子或更好的牌型'.

In horse racing betting contexts, the term 'Place' (as in Win/Place/Show) refers to finishing in the top 2. ALWAYS translate 'Place' as '前二'. NEVER translate it as '位置' (position) or '名次' (rank) without the specific betting term.

In slot machine contexts, 'wheel' (specifically in bonus features) refers to a 'bonus wheel' or '转盘', NOT 'reel' (转轴). 'Wheel slices' or 'slices of the wheel' must be translated as '转盘切片' or '转盘格'. NEVER translate 'wheel' as '转轴' in this context, as '转轴' refers to the slot machine reels.

In casino promotions and marketing copy, the phrase 'here is some place to...' or 'some places to...' when referring to 'ways', 'tips', or 'options' (not physical locations) must be translated as '方法' or '做法'. NEVER translate 'place' as '位置' (physical location).

In casino contexts, 'games' refers to gambling activities or sessions. When referring to a collection or variety, use '游戏选择' or '游戏种类'. NEVER translate 'collection of games' as '游戏收藏' (which implies collecting physical items).

In Three Card Poker, the hand rankings do NOT include 'four of a kind' (四条), 'full house' (葫芦), or 'two pair' (两对). If a sentence mentions 'three' followed by 'of a kind', it refers to '三条' (three of a kind). NEVER translate 'three of a kind' as '四条' or imply the existence of 'four of a kind' in Three Card Poker.

In Pai Gow, when counting pairs, use the classifier '对' correctly: 'two high pairs' = '两对高对', 'two medium pairs' = '两对中对'. NEVER use '一对' (one pair) to describe 'two pairs'. The number before '对' indicates the quantity of pairs.

In poker and casino contexts, 'bet' as a noun (the amount wagered) is '赌注', but the action is '下注'. When referring to specific named bets like 'Play bet', 'Bonus', or 'Trips', keep the English name and add '注' or '旁注' as appropriate (e.g., 'Play注', 'Bonus旁注', 'Trips旁注'). NEVER translate 'Play' as '玩' or 'Bonus' as '奖金' when it is a bet name.

In horse racing exotic bets, the format for terms like 'Exacta', 'Trifecta', 'Superfecta' must be 'English Name（Chinese Translation）' without adding 'bet' or '注' immediately after the parentheses unless part of a larger phrase.

In casino strategy text, 'waiting for the fourth or better flush' (meaning 'four to a flush') must be translated as '有四张同花 (差一张成同花)' or '同花差一张'. NEVER translate literally as '等待第四好的同花' which implies a ranking of flushes.
In bingo contexts, 'game' refers to a session or event, not a software game. ALWAYS translate as '场次' or '活动'. NEVER translate as '游戏' (which implies software).
In casino marketing, 'everyone can win' should be translated as '人人都有机会赢' to avoid the horse racing term '独赢' (win/place/show).
In Pai Gow, 'Joker' is '鬼牌'. NEVER translate as '小丑牌' (clown).
In poker, 'hole cards' are '底牌'. ASR errors like 'whole cards' should be corrected to '底牌'.
In slots, 'payouts' refers to the money won, translated as '赔付'. NEVER translate as '赔率' (odds).
In horse racing, 'box' means betting on any order (复式), and 'straight' means exact order (直选).
In casino promos, 'plan your next lucky trip' should be translated as '开启你的下一次幸运之旅' (natural phrasing) rather than '计划你的下一次幸运之旅' (literal).
In casino promos, 'here is some place to...' meaning 'ways/tips' should be translated as '这里有几个方法' or '这里有几种做法'.
In Pai Gow, 'unless you also have two pair' should be translated as '除非你还有两对'. NEVER combine into '除非你有一对两对'.
In slots, 'reel' = '转轴', 'wheel' (bonus) = '转盘'. 'Wheel slices' = '转盘切片'.
In horse racing, 'Place' = '前二', 'Show' = '前三'.
In slots, 'split symbol' = '分裂符号'. 'Full reel symbol' = '整轴符号'.
In casino promos, 'everyone can win' = '人人都有机会赢'.
In slots, 'wheel' (bonus) = '转盘'.

Recurring mistranslation pattern: when translating content like 'Raise with four cards to a flush as long as you're drawing to the fourth best flush or better.', the model produced '只要你在等待第四张同花或更好的同花，你可以用四张同花听牌加注。' but should have produced '如果你有四张同花（差一张成同花）或更好的牌，可以加注。'. Found 2 similar cases.

In casino and gambling contexts, the term "gaming" refers to gambling activities and must be translated as "游戏" or "游戏活动". NEVER translate it as "赌博" (gambling) or "博彩" (betting) in marketing or general descriptive text, as "gaming" is the industry-standard euphemism for casino entertainment.

When translating "buy-ins" in the context of poker tournaments, use the specific term "买入" (as a noun) or "买入资金" only if explicitly referring to the funds. However, the standard glossary term is "买入". Avoid "投入资金" as it is redundant and less standard.

In casino strategy contexts, the phrase "playing strategies" refers to gambling tactics and should be translated as "玩法策略" or "策略小贴士". NEVER translate it as "游戏策略" (which implies video game software strategy) to maintain domain specificity.

In poker hand rankings, the term "straight" (顺子) is a specific hand ranking higher than "pair" (一对). When translating "straight or better", ALWAYS use "顺子或更好的牌型". NEVER translate "straight" as "一对" (pair) or any other hand ranking.

In poker strategy text, the phrase "waiting for the fourth best flush" or "four to a flush" refers to having four cards of the same suit and needing one more. ALWAYS translate this as "有四张同花 (差一张成同花)" or "同花差一张". NEVER translate literally as "等待第四好的同花" or "等待第四张同花", which implies a ranking of flushes and is nonsensical.

In casino marketing and general text, "everyone can win" should be translated as "人人都有机会赢" to avoid the horse racing term "独赢" (Win). "独赢" is specific to the Win/Place/Show bet type and should not be used in general promotional contexts.

In Pai Gow, when translating 'four of a kind' with specific ranks (e.g., 'four jacks', 'four sixes', 'four sevens through tens'), ALWAYS use the pattern '拿到 [rank/range] 的四条' (e.g., '拿到J的四条', '拿到6的四条', '拿到7到10的四条'). Use the verb '拿到' (get/hand) instead of '有' (have), and use Arabic numerals or letters (J, 6, 7-10) instead of Chinese number words (四个J, 六个).

In slots, 'wheel' (specifically in bonus features) refers to a 'bonus wheel' or '转盘', NOT 'reel' (转轴). 'Wheel slices' or 'slices of the wheel' must be translated as '转盘切片' or '转盘格'. NEVER translate 'wheel' as '转轴' in this context.
In Pai Gow, 'set your hand' / 'set hand' = 摆牌 or 分牌 (arranging cards), NOT 设置手牌. 'split' = 拆分.
For casino promos: 'everyone can win' = '人人都有机会赢' (NOT 每个人都能独赢).

In Ultimate Texas Hold'em (UTH) and similar poker variants, the term "check" refers to the action of folding or declining to make the Play bet (since a check is not a valid action to see a showdown without a Play bet). ALWAYS translate "check" in this specific context as "弃牌" (fold) or "不玩" (do not play). NEVER translate it as "过牌" (check), which implies a passive action in community card games where players can see the next card without betting.

In Pai Gow and poker contexts, when referring to specific card ranks in hand descriptions (e.g., "four sixes", "four jacks", "four sevens through tens"), ALWAYS use the pattern "拿到 [rank/range] 的四条" (e.g., "拿到6的四条", "拿到J的四条", "拿到7到10的四条"). Use the verb "拿到" (get/hand) instead of "有" (have), and use Arabic numerals or letters (6, J, 7-10) instead of Chinese number words (六个, 四个J).
In bingo contexts, the term "game" refers to a session or event, not a software game. ALWAYS translate as "场次" or "活动". NEVER translate as "游戏" (which implies software).
In casino promos, "everyone can win" should be translated as "人人都有机会赢" to avoid the horse racing term "独赢" (win/place/show).
